stem: A industrial engineering study measures the assembly time (seconds) for a sample of 30 factory workers. The sample mean is 323.6 sec with a sample standard deviation of 89.3 sec. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 323.6 sec. 95% CI = [290.3, 356.9]. Point estimate: 323.6 sec.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
